decisions: CMS content stored in structured collections.
Public assets should be delivered through a CDN
Use SSR or ISR for SEO-sensitive pages.
Introduce background worker for scheduled tasks.
Store media in S3-compatible object storage.
Media assets stored in object storage.
Authentication handled via secure session or JWT.
Introduce caching layer for frequently accessed content.

stories.title: Define CMS content model
stories.description: Define CMS collections, media handling, and editorial workflows.

stories.title: Configure CDN
stories.description: Configure CDN for public site asset delivery.

stories.title: Initialize project repository
stories.description: Create project structure using the selected stack.

stories.title: Setup database
stories.description: Configure postgres database and connection layer.

stories.title: Implement authentication
stories.description: Add login, logout and session management.

stories.title: Implement media library
stories.description: Allow uploading and managing media assets.

stories.title: Implement scheduled publishing
stories.description: Create background job to publish content at scheduled time.

stories.title: Setup monitoring and logging
stories.description: Integrate monitoring, logging and error tracking.

stories.title: Implement backups
stories.description: Automate database backups and retention policies.

performance: Public pages should render in under 500ms under normal load.
API responses should target <200ms median latency.
Public assets must be cacheable via CDN.
scalability: System should support horizontal scaling of application servers.
Database must support concurrent editorial users.
security: All authentication flows must enforce secure password hashing.
All external APIs must require authentication or signed requests.
Editorial roles must enforce permission boundaries.
operational: Structured logging should be used for all backend services.

philosophy: Use consistent spacing scale and typography hierarchy.
Minimize cognitive load for editorial workflows.
patterns: Use optimistic UI for publishing actions
Provide clear status indicators for draft vs published content
Preview mode should match public rendering exactly.
recommendations: Use Tailwind CSS or CSS variables for token system.
Maintain a component library shared between admin and public site.
Document component usage and accessibility constraints.

risks.title: Content schema evolution
risks.risk: Content models may evolve after production launch.
risks.impact: Database migrations and data compatibility issues.
risks.mitigation: Introduce schema versioning and migration strategy.

risks.title: Media storage growth
risks.mitigation: Use S3-compatible storage with lifecycle rules.

risks.title: Traffic spikes
risks.risk: Public sites may experience sudden traffic increases.
risks.mitigation: Use CDN caching and edge delivery.

risks.title: API coupling
risks.risk: Frontend tightly coupled to CMS API.
risks.mitigation: Introduce caching layer or static generation.
